what_it_does: This file is a terrain-entry ledger record that describes an external two-file folder (/home/tim/artefacts) containing two standalone founding-arc documents, summarizing their contents, provenance, relationships to other entries, read coverage, and current status.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares type terrain-entry, register descriptive, status unconfirmed, relation external, kind work, and no git remote.
Lists two contents: Vi.md (81K vision narrative) and model-survey-2026-05-26.md (23K verbatim model-survey transcript).
Relates to [[corpora]] and [[foundation]] via wiki-style links.
Includes exact file mtimes, sizes, and a coverage block showing 2 of 2 files read but bodies only skimmed.
Model survey cites a specific session ID and source transcript paths with line numbers/timestamps.
Tags are [model, voice] despite the folder also containing a vision narrative.
Notes state is dormant and warns the RTX 4080 survey may be stale.
standouts: Explicit coverage caveat: both file heads read, but the 81K Vi.md body was only skimmed.
Model-survey provenance is traced back to a specific session transcript (session ID 12c59b4e-…) and a superpowers archive snapshot.
Vi.md is framed as a partial, AI-written vision narrative addressed simultaneously to 'the origin' and a future collaborator/backer/participant.
Status is unconfirmed even though coverage says both files were read.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry schema with YAML frontmatter and a structured markdown body.
Uses wiki-style [[...]] cross-references for related entries.
Includes provenance metadata (session IDs, transcript paths, mtimes) inline.
Breaks typical 'complete' ledger convention by explicitly documenting incomplete read coverage and unconfirmed status.
concerns: Status 'unconfirmed' is not explained despite the entry claiming both files were read.
Coverage caveat means the summary of Vi.md may miss significant portions of the 81K body.
Absolute external path /home/tim/artefacts and no git remote make reproducibility/location fragile.
Model-survey content is flagged as potentially stale relative to the current stack.
notes: A descriptive ledger card for two founding-arc documents, useful as a provenance stub but explicitly incomplete on Vi.md coverage.
questions: What criteria keep this entry's status as 'unconfirmed' after both files were read?
What is the larger system or organism that Vi.md describes as a 'partial view'?
Which specific local/cloud models in the survey are still part of the current Company stack?
Why are the tags limited to 'model' and 'voice' when the folder also contains a vision narrative?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a descriptive terrain-entry for the artefacts folder and does exactly that—summarizing contents, provenance, and relationships.
intention_signals: To create a stable, discoverable index that anchors two founding documents in the orienteering ledger, links them to related terrain, and is transparent about how thoroughly they were read and how current they are.
novelty: The detailed provenance chain for the model survey—down to session ID, transcript path, line numbers, and timestamps—is unusually precise for a static document entry; the dual-reader framing of Vi.md is also distinctive.
contribution: Provides a compact descriptive index and provenance record for two founding-arc documents, including content summaries, dates, sizes, read-coverage status, and related-entry links, so a fused system can locate and contextualize them without re-reading the sources.
summary_for_embedding: A terrain-entry ledger record describing the external /home/tim/artefacts folder, which holds the Vi.md vision narrative and a 2026-05-26 RTX 4080 model-survey transcript, along with their provenance, coverage status, and links to related entries.
intention_for_embedding: To index and contextualize two founding-arc reference documents while being explicit about their read coverage and potential staleness.
